name: Default Go
description: Builtin Go workflow contract for project setup, branch planning, implementation, closeout, and cleanup.

node_templates.description_markdown: Project-level Go workflow contract with explicit setup before normal branch execution.

project_metadata_defaults.standards_markdown:
- Bare control repo with visible sibling worktrees.
- One dedicated `gopls` MCP server per active worktree.
- Use Hylla MCP first for Tillsyn code understanding; use other tools only as needed.
- Use Context7 and `go doc` during planning before building, before fixing tests after failures, and during QA.
- For semantic, high-risk, or ambiguous work, record a semi-formal reasoning certificate covering premises, evidence, trace or cases, conclusion, and unknowns.
- Use Hylla for committed repo-local evidence, use git diff for uncommitted local deltas, and use Context7 only for external semantics the repo cannot prove.
- During project setup or template refresh decisions, compare current Hylla state with the currently installed DB template/binding state and ask the dev before running DB-mutating updates such as builtin ensure, template reapply, or similar refresh steps.
- Shared cross-client named agents should stay aligned across Codex and Claude: `orchestration-agent`, `planning-agent`, `research-agent`, `builder-agent`, `qa-proof-agent`, `qa-falsification-agent`, `closeout-agent`, `commit-and-reingest-agent`, and `gopls-worktree-agent`.
- Build in small tested increments and prefer TDD where practical.
- Coverage expectations remain real gates; maintain the repo minimum and use the canonical `mage` verification path.
- Prefer the smallest concrete design that satisfies the current requirement.
- Reuse or refactor existing code when that is the best option instead of adding speculative structure.
- Do not add abstraction for hypothetical future variation.
- Prefer idiomatic Go naming, package structure, interface placement, error handling, and test shape.
- Wrap and bubble errors with context and keep logging at meaningful operational boundaries instead of deep utility layers.
- Unresolved uncertainty must become explicit coordination state such as comments, handoffs, or attention instead of optimistic completion.
- Confirmed-good build work must be committed and refreshed into Hylla before downstream reasoning treats the graph as current.
- MCP-first dogfooding for runtime and operator workflows.
- `mage` is the canonical build/test gate.
- Laslig styling for all Mage functions and CLI output.
- Fang for help and CLI command surfaces.
- Bubble Tea v2, Bubbles v2, Lip Gloss v2, and Charmbracelet stack on v2.
- GitHub Actions CI and release snapshot checks must stay green.
- No ad-hoc `.codex/` directories inside worktrees.
- Follow `AGENTS.md` workflow and worktree rules.
- Comments and handoffs are the coordination layer.
- Template-generated blockers must be completed truthfully before done.
- Use `depends_on`, `blocked_by`, and `blocked_reason` to express prerequisite ordering until richer workflow ordering rules land.
- Never use another agent's auth or `auth_context_id`, and never paste auth material into comments, handoffs, tasks, or docs.
- Orchestrators own child auth cleanup and must leave no stale sessions, leases, or coordination trash behind.

child_rules.description_template: Project-only onboarding work for template fit, Hylla decisions, metadata lock, and preparation for the first branch lane.

node_templates.description_markdown: Project-only onboarding phase that must happen before normal branch work starts.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Bootstrap the project, confirm template fit, compare Hylla and DB state, and prepare the first branch lane.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Template fit reviewed, Hylla decisions recorded, current Hylla versus DB state reviewed with the dev before any DB update, project metadata locked, and the first branch lane plus PLAN phase are explicitly queued.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Confirm standards, Hylla freshness, current DB template/binding state, and the initial workflow shape with the dev before broad implementation or DB-mutating refresh actions.

child_rules.description_template: Review whether default-go still matches this project's needs before normal execution begins.
Choose `structural_only`, `embeddings_only`, or `full_enrichment` with the dev.
Run the initial Hylla ingest or refresh for the intended repo state.
Confirm the Hylla artifact is current to the intended git ref.
Compare current Hylla state with the currently installed DB template and binding state, then ask the dev before applying any DB-mutating update or refresh.
Lock project metadata, standards, auth hygiene expectations, and coordination rules before normal execution.

child_rules.title_template: CREATE OR CONFIRM FIRST BRANCH LANE
child_rules.description_template: Create or confirm the first visible sibling worktree branch lane and its dedicated gopls MCP entry.

child_rules.title_template: CREATE OR CONFIRM FIRST PLAN PHASE
child_rules.description_template: Queue the first branch lane so its PLAN phase is explicit before broad implementation starts.

node_templates.description_markdown: A branch/worktree lane that auto-generates the normal PLAN -> BUILD -> CLOSEOUT -> BRANCH CLEANUP phases.

child_rules.description_template: Planning phase for current-state understanding, task shaping, and validation design before BUILD starts.
Implementation phase for concrete build-task work.
Verification, review, and readiness phase after BUILD.
Final cleanup phase for branch/worktree/MCP retirement once closeout is truly done.

node_templates.description_markdown: Explicit planning phase that should exist before broad implementation begins. `planning-agent` and `research-agent` work here to ground the plan in Hylla evidence, check reuse opportunities, prefer the smallest viable design, and leave semantic or high-risk work with a written reasoning certificate plus proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA preparation before BUILD starts.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Understand current state, gather repo-local and external evidence, identify reuse or refactor opportunities, and shape the executable task tree before BUILD begins.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Current state is understood, scope is confirmed, implementation tasks exist, validation is defined, prerequisites are explicit, semantic or high-risk claims are supported by a written reasoning certificate with evidence and explicit unknowns, and the plan reflects the smallest viable idiomatic Go design rather than speculative abstraction.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla first for repo-local behavior, Context7 and `go doc` only for external semantics the repo cannot prove, record counterexample checks, identify proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA expectations, and validate the planned task tree with the dev before BUILD.

completion_checklist.text: Relevant Hylla evidence gathered and recorded for the planned scope.
External documentation gathered only where repo evidence is insufficient.
Scope and intended operator outcome confirmed.
Build task tree created for BUILD.
Validation plan includes proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA checks.
Semantic or high-risk claims summarized in task evidence or a reasoning comment.
The plan checks reuse, YAGNI pressure, Go idioms, test strategy, and incremental delivery expectations.

child_rules.description_template: Have `research-agent` or `planning-agent` trace the relevant symbols, callers, data flow, invariants, and reuse candidates through Hylla before local implementation begins.
Have `research-agent` gather only the external and package-local documentation needed for semantics the repository itself cannot prove.
Confirm the actual scope and intended operator outcome before implementation.

Create the detailed build-task and supporting task tree for the upcoming BUILD phase, making semantic-risk work decomposable, verifiable, incremental, and assignable to `builder-agent` plus the correct QA agents.

Define how the work will be validated before handoff and closeout, including proof-oriented QA, falsification-oriented QA, counterexamples, alternate traces, likely hidden dependencies, and Go-quality checks.
Plan or confirm visible sibling worktree setup, dedicated gopls MCP wiring, and branch-lane basics.
Define closeout, auth cleanup, branch cleanup, and coordination expectations up front.

node_templates.description_markdown: Implementation phase where concrete `build-task` items should live. `builder-agent` should implement in small tested increments, prefer idiomatic concrete Go structure, and avoid speculative abstraction.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Implement the planned changes using concrete build-task items and the smallest justified design.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Planned build tasks are completed truthfully, semantic claims match the final code, Go naming and structure are idiomatic, speculative abstraction is avoided, and all generated QA blockers are resolved.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Run the planned package and repo checks, reconcile any semantic claims against the final implemented code, and make sure the implementation stays incremental, testable, and idiomatic before handing work to closeout.

completion_checklist.text: Implementation matches the task objective.
Changed semantics are reflected in task evidence.
Open unknowns or risks are explicit instead of implied away.
Generated QA blockers are positioned to verify the actual claim and challenge unnecessary complexity.
Implementation preserves Go idioms for naming, package structure, interfaces, tests, errors, and logging boundaries.

node_templates.description_markdown: Post-build verification and readiness phase. `closeout-agent` should reconcile final code, final Hylla state, proof-oriented QA findings, falsification-oriented QA findings, and any recorded reasoning before declaring the lane ready for handoff, push, or cleanup.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Verify the completed build work, attach evidence, reconcile reasoning with final code state, and confirm readiness for push, PR, or handoff.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Checks are green, Hylla is current to final git state, review/testing happened, semantic claims still match the final code, justified simplicity and Go-quality expectations still hold, unresolved risks are explicit, and the branch lane is ready for cleanup.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use the completed work plus Hylla-backed understanding to run proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA, reconcile reasoning against final git state, and escalate remaining uncertainty instead of forcing readiness.

completion_checklist.text: Required mage gates completed.
Hylla refreshed and current to the final git state.
Proof-oriented QA verified evidence completeness and design support.
Falsification-oriented QA challenged the conclusion and unnecessary complexity.
Open risks or unknowns are explicit.
Readiness is based on evidence rather than optimism.

child_rules.description_template: Run the required `mage` checks and record the green result.
Create or verify the local commit that captures the completed branch work.

child_rules.title_template: HYLLA REFRESHED AND CURRENT TO GIT
child_rules.description_template: Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` or the freshness-focused research flow refresh the Hylla artifact as needed, confirm it matches the current git state, and reconcile recorded reasoning against the refreshed graph.
Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, contract coverage, Go idiom fit, testability, and justified simplicity using task state plus Hylla-backed understanding.
Have `qa-falsification-agent` attempt falsification through counterexamples, alternate traces, hidden dependencies, contract mismatches, YAGNI pressure, and unnecessary abstraction checks.
Review the completed change with the dev before cleanup or push.
Run the final collaborative testing pass with the dev before declaring readiness.
Confirm the lane is ready for push, PR, or explicit handoff only when checks, evidence, Hylla state, and risk handling all support readiness without leaving coordination trash behind.

node_templates.description_markdown: Final cleanup phase for branch/worktree/MCP retirement.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Retire the finished lane cleanly without leaving stale branches, worktrees, gopls entries, sessions, leases, or coordination rows behind.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Branch/worktree/MCP cleanup is complete and no stale auth or coordination trash remains.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Confirm closeout is truly done first, then verify local git and MCP state after cleanup.

child_rules.description_template: Confirm the closeout work is actually complete before branch retirement begins.
Remove the visible sibling worktree for the finished lane when it is no longer needed.
Remove the branch when it is no longer needed.
Remove the lane-specific gopls MCP entry from the bare-root Codex config.
Rerun `codex mcp list` and refresh the visible MCP registry after cleanup.
Confirm the old lane server is gone and no stale auth, lease, or coordination rows remain.

node_templates.description_markdown: Concrete implementation task that auto-generates distinct proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA subtasks plus a commit-and-reingest closeout step.

task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The planned change is implemented truthfully, semantic claims match the final code, Go quality expectations still hold, generated QA blockers are resolved, and confirmed-good work is committed and refreshed into Hylla.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Run the required package and repo checks, verify semantic claims with proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA, then commit and refresh Hylla before handing this task to closeout.

completion_checklist.text: Implementation matches the task objective.
QA proof review verified evidence completeness and design support.
QA falsification review challenged the conclusion and unnecessary complexity.
Confirmed-good work is committed and Hylla is refreshed to that commit.

child_rules.description_template: Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, claimed behavior support, Go naming and structure fit, error and logging discipline, testability, and justified simplicity for the parent build task.

Have `qa-falsification-agent` try to falsify the conclusion through counterexamples, alternate traces, hidden dependencies, contract mismatches, YAGNI pressure, and unnecessary abstraction checks for the parent build task.

Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` commit confirmed-good work, trigger Hylla refresh, wait for the artifact to catch up to that commit, and record the resulting freshness evidence for downstream reasoning.
